name: Saitama Prefecture (Japan)

colors.symbolism: purity, peace and the bright future of Saitama
the rising sun, vitality and the ardour of the people of Saitama

description: A white field charged at the centre with a red emblem composed of sixteen magatama (comma-shaped beads) radiating in a circular sun-like pattern.
history: Saitama's prefectural symbol was selected by public competition and adopted on 1 September 1964 to mark the centenary of the Meiji restoration. The flag, with the red emblem on a white field, has been in use since the same year.

features.symbolism: purity and the limitless promise of the prefecture

features.role: Saitama prefectural emblem (sixteen magatama in a sun pattern)
features.symbolism: sixteen magatama (comma-shaped beads) arranged in a radiating circle. The number sixteen evokes the sun and prosperity; magatama recall the ancient artefacts unearthed in the region. The combined form is read as the sun rising over Saitama and the harmony and growth of the prefecture.
